regimeNotes: NEUTRAL regime maintained 2026-06-21 (Sunday, re-run). Stock prices held at June 18 close — June 19 was Juneteenth market holiday; June 20-21 are weekend; next trading day is Monday June 22. SPY $746.74, NVDA $210.69, MSFT $379.40, GOOGL $368.03, UNH $400.96 (all unchanged since last review). Crypto slightly softer vs earlier run: BTC $64,169 (-$69 vs $64,238 earlier), ETH $1,731.08 (-$8.65), SOL $73.90 (+$0.62). F&G index 23 (Extreme Fear, unchanged). BTC dominance 56.22%, total crypto mcap $2.29T (+0.62% 24h). 10Y yield 4.44-4.45% (elevated, restrictive). DXY ~100.79 (slightly easier, mild tailwind for risk). EUR/USD ~1.1467. Fed: hawkish stance maintained. HOLD day — 0 trades. Crypto sleeve at 20.03% NAV (at neutral 20% cap — no room to add). All major stock positions near 15% NAV entry cap — not a fear-driven entry. Cash 13.23% NAV. NAV $100,107.30 (down $23.61 vs earlier run due to crypto softness). Next catalysts: PCE data June 25, first trading day back Monday June 22.

SPY.thesis: Core US equity beta ($746.74, 13.07% NAV). S&P near all-time highs with AI/semiconductor rally sustaining broad index. Diversified anchor under the growth-tilted mandate; participates in any continued risk-on leg.
SPY.invalidationLevel: Sustained close below 660 USD or confirmed Fed re-pricing toward multiple hikes pushing SPY into a technical downtrend.

NVDA.thesis: AI/compute secular leader at $210.69 (above $208.44 avgCost, ~14.64% NAV). Data-center revenue and hyperscaler capex still accelerating. Highest-conviction growth driver for the 33% CAGR target.
NVDA.invalidationLevel: AI capex digestion or data-center demand rollover; close below ~150 USD.

MSFT.thesis: Quality AI/cloud compounder at $379.40 (below $391.98 avgCost, ~14.43% NAV). Broad market conditions affecting quality names; cloud+AI thesis intact and FCF durable. Watching for recovery toward 390+.
MSFT.invalidationLevel: Cloud growth deceleration or AI capex without demonstrable ROI; close below 340 USD.

GOOGL.thesis: Quality growth (search + cloud + AI) at $368.03 (above $366.53 avgCost, ~14.44% NAV). Near-monopoly search funds AI optionality; resilient earnings in hawkish-rate tape.
GOOGL.invalidationLevel: Search share erosion to AI rivals; close below 310 USD.

UNH.thesis: Defensive value at $400.96 (below $408.52 avgCost, ~5.88% NAV). Medical-cost and regulatory headwinds persist; retained as defensive ballast. Analyst targets 450-466 USD give upside.
UNH.invalidationLevel: Further regulatory or medical-cost shock; close below 360 USD.

GLD.thesis: Tail/debasement hedge at $387.12 (above $386.54 avgCost, ~4.27% NAV). Central-bank buying supportive; small size prevents drag on 33% CAGR target. DXY ~100.79 easing slightly.
GLD.invalidationLevel: Real yields spike with a strong USD reversal; close below 350 USD.

BTC.thesis: Core crypto sleeve leader at $64,169 (below $64,629 avgCost, ~14.38% NAV). F&G 23 (Extreme Fear), BTC dominance 56.22% stable. Minor softness from earlier run, well above invalidation. Positioned for the next risk-on expansion to 35% sleeve cap.

ETH.thesis: Discounted L1 leader at $1,731.08 (above $1,667.47 avgCost, ~4.15% NAV). Dominance 9.13% remains historically low = asymmetric upside when risk-on returns. Held within the 20% crypto sleeve.
ETH.invalidationLevel: Structural loss of share to L1 rivals; close below 1,300 USD.

SOL.thesis: High-beta satellite at $73.90 (above $67.81 avgCost, ~1.50% NAV). +3.3% 24h shows strong beta in a recovering crypto tape. First position to scale up when sleeve cap rises to 35% on a risk-on regime flip.
SOL.invalidationLevel: Break below 52 USD; network deterioration or sentiment collapse.
